clusters.title: Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" as meetings with China's Xi wrap up
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
bullet_summary: The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported
Xi’s remarks, published by China’s foreign ministry after his two-hour meeting with Trump on Thursday morning, said Taiwan was “the most important issue in China-US relations”

items.title: Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" as meetings with China's Xi wrap up
items.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are concluding their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.
items.content: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are concluding their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.

items.title: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.
items.content: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.

items.title: Trump hails ‘fantastic trade deals’ with Xi, China issues warning on Taiwan
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump touted economic wins ahead of his final talks with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping on Friday, claiming the two made “fantastic trade deals”. The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported.
items.content: President Donald Trump touted economic wins ahead of his final talks with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping on Friday, claiming the two made “fantastic trade deals”. The pomp and warm words during Trump’s visit were overshadowed by Xi’s blunt warning that US missteps on Taiwan could push the two countries into conflict, Chinese media reported.

items.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help
items.standfirst: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.
items.content: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

items.title: For anxious Taiwan, Trump’s silence after Xi talks is best possible outcome
items.standfirst: Island breathes sigh of relief as fears recede that US could jettison longstanding supportBefore this week’s summit between the Chinese and US presidents, Taiwan had been cast as the anxious bystander.Observers suggested that Taipei feared the unpredictable and transactional Donald Trump might overturn Washington’s longstanding support for the island democracy, which Beijing claims as a breakaway province, during Thursday and Friday’s talks. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: President Trump was welcomed by President Xi Jinping in Beijing for what will be a whirlwind tour.

items.title: Xi warns Trump of possible conflict over Taiwan at grand Beijing summit
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned of “clashes and even conflicts” with the U.S. over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned of “clashes and even conflicts” with the U.S. over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies.

items.title: Watch: What happened on day one of Trump's China visit?
items.standfirst: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.
items.content: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.

clusters.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Raul Castro's grandson in Havana, US and Cuban officials say
clusters.neutral_headline: CIA director has met officials in Havana for talks, Cuba claims
bullet_summary: The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations, with the aim of contributing to the political dialogue between both nations”, a statement said
CIA Director John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday in a bid to improve dialogue between Washington and the communist-run island, the Cuban government said
The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations", Havana said

items.title: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as a ship is seized and another is sunk
items.standfirst: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman.
items.content: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman.(Image credit: Digital Embed)

items.title: Iran says ships entering strait of Hormuz must cooperate after vessel seized
items.standfirst: Docked ship reportedly seized outside UAE port by “unauthorised personnel”The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has said ships entering the strait of Hormuz must cooperate with the Iranian navy as reports emerged of a ship being seized outside a United Arab Emirate port and taken towards Iranian waters.The UK Maritime Trading Organisation said the docked ship was seized by “unauthorised personnel” while it was anchored off the coast of the United Arab Emirates port of Fujairah near the southern entry to the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Labour, u ok hun? - podcast
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories, France24

items.title: Labour, u ok hun? - podcast
items.standfirst: Guardian columnist Rafael Behr talks through a tumultuous day for Labour and Keir Starmer – following the resignation of health secretary Wes Streeting, and the renewed possibility of a Westminster comeback for Andy BurnhamAfter a week of speculation and gossip, Thursday seemed to be the day the Labour leadership contest really took off. Officially though, Keir Starmer is still in place … and not going anywhere.Guardian columnist Rafael Behr talks through a frantic day that started with Wes Streeting’s resignation as health secretary and ended with the very real possibility of his rival – Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham – returning to parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Guardian columnist Rafael Behr talks through a tumultuous day for Labour and Keir Starmer – following the resignation of health secretary Wes Streeting, and the renewed possibility of a Westminster comeback for Andy BurnhamAfter a week of speculation and gossip, Thursday seemed to be the day the Labour leadership contest really took off. Officially though, Keir Starmer is still in place … and not going anywhere.Guardian columnist Rafael Behr talks through a frantic day that started with Wes Streeting’s resignation as health secretary and ended with the very real possibility of his rival – Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham – returning to parliament. Continue reading...

items.title: Watch: The day Labour's potential leadership race began to heat up
items.standfirst: Political turmoil continued in Westminster on Thursday after Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned Starmer's government saying he had "lost confidence" in his leadership.
items.content: Political turmoil continued in Westminster on Thursday after Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned Starmer's government saying he had "lost confidence" in his leadership.

items.title: Labour politicians jockey for possible challenge to UK's Starmer
items.standfirst: British ​Labour lawmaker Josh Simons on Thursday said he would resign from his seat in parliament in a move ​designed ‌to give Greater Manchester Mayor Andy ⁠Burnham a chance to return to parliament and challenge Prime Minister ‌Keir Starmer. FRANCE 24's Béédicte Paviot reports from 10 Downing Street.
items.content: British ​Labour lawmaker Josh Simons on Thursday said he would resign from his seat in parliament in a move ​designed ‌to give Greater Manchester Mayor Andy ⁠Burnham a chance to return to parliament and challenge Prime Minister ‌Keir Starmer. FRANCE 24's Béédicte Paviot reports from 10 Downing Street.

items.content: He quits as health secretary after days of speculation that he is preparing to launch a leadership challenge against Sir Keir Starmer.

clusters.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World, NPR News

items.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.
items.content: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

items.title: Supreme court allows abortion pill mifepristone to continue to be available by mail
items.standfirst: Louisiana had sued the FDA in a bid to curtail the regulatory agency’s rules on prescribing mifepristone remotelySign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court upheld nationwide access to mail-order mifepristone, an abortion medication, in a shadow-docket decision on Thursday.Louisiana sued the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in October in a bid to curtail the regulatory agency’s rules on prescribing mifepristone remotely, arguing that it interfered with the state’s ban on abortion. Continue reading...

items.title: The Supreme Court keeps abortion pill mifepristone available by telehealth
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.
items.content: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.(Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)

clusters.title: Abu Dhabi 'doesn't want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel', analyst says
clusters.neutral_headline: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
bullet_summary: Earlier Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-U
“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night
Benjamin Netanyahu's office said he secretly visited the United Arab Emirates earlier this year during the US-Israeli war with Iran - but the UAE has denied the trip took place

items.title: Abu Dhabi 'doesn't want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel', analyst says
items.standfirst: A renewed escalation in the Strait of Hormuz saw the seizure on Thursday of a ship anchored off the Unites Arab Emirates coast. Earlier Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-U.S. war with Iran, though the UAE swiftly denied that any secret visit had occurred. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Federico Donelli, Assistant Professor of International Relations at the University of Trieste, says that "Abu Dhabi does not want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel in the face of what Israel is doing in the region".
items.content: A renewed escalation in the Strait of Hormuz saw the seizure on Thursday of a ship anchored off the Unites Arab Emirates coast. Earlier Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had announced he had quietly visited the UAE during the Israeli-U.S. war with Iran, though the UAE swiftly denied that any secret visit had occurred. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Federico Donelli, Assistant Professor of International Relations at the University of Trieste, says that "Abu Dhabi does not want to appear too much in cooperation with Israel in the face of what Israel is doing in the region".

items.title: Iran accuses United Arab Emirates of being 'active partner' in US-Israeli war
items.standfirst: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the United Arab Emirates on Thursday of playing an active role in the US-Israeli war against his country. Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi denied took place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
items.content: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the United Arab Emirates on Thursday of playing an active role in the US-Israeli war against his country. Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi denied took place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: France squad: Didier Deschamps unveils his selections for the 2026 World Cup
clusters.neutral_headline: Mbappe and Dembele head up star-studded France World Cup squad
bullet_summary: Didier Deschamps has announced his 26-man France squad for the 2026 World Cup

items.title: France squad: Didier Deschamps unveils his selections for the 2026 World Cup
items.standfirst: Didier Deschamps has announced his 26-man France squad for the 2026 World Cup. For his final tournament in charge of Les Bleus, the France coach has selected 25 capped internationals and one newcomer, Robin Risser. France will travel with three goalkeepers, nine defenders, five midfielders and nine forwards.
items.content: Didier Deschamps has announced his 26-man France squad for the 2026 World Cup. For his final tournament in charge of Les Bleus, the France coach has selected 25 capped internationals and one newcomer, Robin Risser. France will travel with three goalkeepers, nine defenders, five midfielders and nine forwards.

items.title: Mbappé heads up France squad in Deschamps' final World Cup bid
items.standfirst: Kylian Mbappé will lead France’s bid for World Cup glory after coach Didier Deschamps named a largely unchanged 26-man squad Thursday for this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Mbappé, recovering from a thigh injury suffered during Real Madrid’s title run-in, is still expected to captain the team.
items.content: Kylian Mbappé will lead France’s bid for World Cup glory after coach Didier Deschamps named a largely unchanged 26-man squad Thursday for this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Mbappé, recovering from a thigh injury suffered during Real Madrid’s title run-in, is still expected to captain the team.

items.content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday said President Xi Jinping had offered China's help to open the Strait of Hormuz and pledged not to send military equipment to aid Iran in its war against the US and Israel.
